title: aeschynanthus argentii
intro: Aeschynanthus argentii, also known as the 'Whitingi', is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Gesneriaceae family. It is primarily found in New Zealand and has been used in traditional Maori medicine for centuries. The plant is believed to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Traditional herbal preparations of Aeschynanthus argentii include teas, infusions, and topical applications, which are used to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
